jokes.text: I enjoy bicentennials as much as the next guy, Mom, but we're not doing that.
jokes.setup: Lucille wants to throw the most lavish birthday party the town has ever seen
jokes.punchline: Michael treating a birthday party like it's a 200-year celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael - dry, logical response to absurd situation with unexpected word choice

jokes.text: Buster had been avoiding Lucille Austero since he accidentally flirted with her while not wearing his glasses
jokes.setup: Simple explanation for why Buster is avoiding someone
jokes.punchline: The absurdly innocent reason - no glasses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster characterization - innocent action with awkward consequences

jokes.text: And then accidentally entered her apartment, thinking it was his mother's
jokes.setup: Series of 'accidents'
jokes.punchline: Most intimate 'accident' yet, with Oedipal implications
jokes.explanation: Third escalation with perfect psychological insight into Buster's mother issues

jokes.text: Oh, here comes the 10:15 conniption, right on time.
jokes.setup: Buster having what appears to be a panic attack
jokes.punchline: It's so regular she can set her watch by it
jokes.explanation: Great specificity and word choice - 'conniption' is perfectly old-fashioned for Lucille 2

jokes.text: Get away from that stove. You're going to light your hair on fire.
jokes.setup: Lucille just said she has no idea where Buster gets his anxiety from
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates the source with her own paranoid warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction showing Lucille's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: He's weak.
jokes.setup: After witnessing Buster's complete emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: Lucille's understated assessment
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly understated given the extreme nature of what just happened. Pure Lucille.

jokes.text: You're my third least-favorite child.
jokes.setup: Michael refusing to let her have parties
jokes.punchline: Casually cruel ranking system for her children
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Lucille - mathematically precise cruelty delivered casually

jokes.text: Michael came home to find his mother's fourth least-favorite child
jokes.setup: Previous ranking of Michael as third least-favorite
jokes.punchline: Immediate callback identifying Gob's even lower ranking
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback that expands the cruel ranking system

jokes.text: Well, they got the Asian right. 'Hotties' might be a stretch.
jokes.setup: Asian couple looking at the yacht described as 'Asian hotties'
jokes.punchline: Gob's casually inappropriate assessment
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob inappropriateness but not particularly clever or surprising

jokes.text: Well, let's start with the little one.
jokes.setup: Continuing evaluation of the Asian couple
jokes.punchline: Even more inappropriate objectification
jokes.explanation: Continues the cringe but doesn't add much beyond establishing Gob's character

jokes.text: Michael, do you have any idea what would happen if I were to visit prison? - Hmm. - It would be pandemonium.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting Lindsay visit their father in jail
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's delusional self-importance about causing prison chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay vanity - imagining prisoners going wild over her presence

jokes.text: Yeah, I got some of that... except he also didn't like my looks.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining their father only values her looks
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry observation that he got the worst of both worlds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delivery - turning family dysfunction into deadpan self-roast

jokes.text: Come on. That would never happen. - She can't drive herself. - I mean, she's the world's worst driver.
jokes.setup: Michael saying taking their mother to dinner would never happen
jokes.punchline: The reason isn't what we expect - it's her terrible driving
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that sets up the driving subplot

jokes.text: Lucille had recently been featured on Fox's World's Worst Drivers
jokes.setup: Lindsay saying their mother is the world's worst driver
jokes.punchline: It's literally true - she was on TV for it
jokes.explanation: Great escalation making the hyperbole literal with perfect show reference

jokes.text: She didn't. I dummied her up a new one. Not my best work though. She wanted to look 48. I nearly airbrushed her into oblivion. We ended up checking 'Albino' on the form.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Lucille's driver's license
jokes.punchline: Gob's fake ID operation with absurd over-airbrushing results
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect visual absurdity - airbrushing someone into albinism

jokes.text: She was having trouble even lifting up this bag of groceries.
jokes.setup: Lindsay describing their frail mother
jokes.punchline: Sets up later reveal that 'groceries' were gold bars
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for later payoff, works better on rewatch

jokes.text: For Gangy? - Yeah, how did you know? - She called and invited us.
jokes.setup: Michael planning a surprise party
jokes.punchline: It's not a surprise - she organized it herself
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom setup subversion - surprise party that isn't surprising

jokes.text: Come on. This is a Bluth family celebration. It's no place for children.
jokes.setup: Kids asking if they have to go to the party
jokes.punchline: Michael's dark assessment of family gatherings as inappropriate for kids
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark humor about dysfunctional family dynamics

jokes.text: Gob's got the charm, Buster's got the... - High-fastening pants. - Have you said that? No. I'm saying that now.
jokes.setup: Lindsay listing what each child is known for
jokes.punchline: George Sr. makes up something absurdly specific for Buster on the spot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvised put-down with perfect specificity to Buster's character

jokes.text: Lindsay was surprised once again that no heads were turned
jokes.setup: Lindsay expecting to cause a scene in prison with her looks
jokes.punchline: Complete indifference from inmates despite her outfit
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Lindsay's vanity with visual comedy

jokes.text: It was a love between two cousins that the world thought was wrong. But it was the world that was wrong.
jokes.setup: Romantic movie voiceover style narration
jokes.punchline: Applied to cousin relationship in a way that sounds like justification
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of romantic movie tropes applied to taboo relationship

jokes.text: You strike one more person, and it's technically a spree.
jokes.setup: Discussing Lucille's driving record
jokes.punchline: Technical definition of vehicular violence spree
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect legal/criminal terminology precision

jokes.text: And she was surprised that her family didn't show up
jokes.setup: Surprise party for Lucille
jokes.punchline: She's surprised by the surprise - that nobody came
jokes.explanation: Solid ironic twist on surprise party expectations

jokes.text: In fact, Buster was worried about running into Lucille 2. So he never left his mother's apartment. Lucille 2 was also supposed to go, but was waiting until she saw Buster emerge.
jokes.setup: Both avoiding each other
jokes.punchline: Perfect standoff where neither can leave
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy setup with perfect psychological logic

jokes.text: What frail act got you this time, Michael? She couldn't open a jar? What, did a clump of her hair come out in your hands?
jokes.setup: Gob knowing Michael falls for their mother's manipulation
jokes.punchline: Specific examples of Lucille's fake frailty acts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob insight into family dynamics with gross specificity

jokes.text: I've been a horrible mother. - No, Mom. You're great. You've been a great mother.
jokes.setup: Lucille admitting the truth about her parenting
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately contradicting what they all know is true
jokes.explanation: Classic enablement comedy with Michael's automatic people-pleasing

jokes.text: Um, is it okay if I do it for Mom and not you? - Yeah. - 'Cause I really like Mom.
jokes.setup: Michael asking George Michael to help with party for him
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent but hurtful honesty
jokes.explanation: Sweet cringe moment showing George Michael's pure honesty hurting Michael

jokes.text: Yes. I'm foolish and I'm funny and I'm needy. - No. Are you sure I'm not needy? 'Cause I feel needy sometimes.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 listing her flaws
jokes.punchline: Buster fixating only on neediness, revealing his own issues
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing both characters' neuroses

jokes.text: You and I. Is it 'I' or is it 'me'? - Buster, it's us. - Yes. It is us. Us.
jokes.setup: Grammar confusion in romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Buster's relief at simple solution, repeated 'us'
jokes.explanation: Sweet awkward moment with grammatical anxiety turned romantic

jokes.text: And once again, no one showed up.
jokes.setup: Second surprise party attempt
jokes.punchline: Exact same result as before
jokes.explanation: Effective repetition that emphasizes the family's consistent neglect

jokes.text: I let you drive, didn't I?
jokes.setup: Michael listing what he did for his mother
jokes.punchline: The ultimate sacrifice was letting her drive (given her record)
jokes.explanation: Good callback to driving subplot with proper emphasis on danger

jokes.text: It's an idiot on a scooter at night? It's gotta be Gob.
jokes.setup: Spotting someone on a scooter
jokes.punchline: Lucille's immediate, accurate identification based on stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille logic and casual cruelty toward Gob

jokes.text: I was thrown clear. I only care about you.
jokes.setup: Car accident concern
jokes.punchline: Lucille's dramatic declaration while Michael is the one injured
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille self-centeredness disguised as concern

jokes.text: 'Scoot'? Scooter. Was there a scooter involved?
jokes.setup: Doctor telling Michael to scoot up in bed
jokes.punchline: Word triggers memory of accident involving Gob's scooter
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that advances plot and shows memory returning

jokes.text: It was a wild, wild ride.
jokes.setup: Describing kissing Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Buster's innocent but sexual description
jokes.explanation: Good Buster innocence with unintended sexual overtones

jokes.text: My God, for the first time in years I felt like I was standing on solid ground.
jokes.setup: Buster describing his confidence with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Metaphor particularly meaningful for anxiety-ridden Buster
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with metaphor perfect for Buster's issues

jokes.text: Totally cool. Didn't feel a thing. You know, Mom, I'm crazy about this aspirin. I can't believe we give it to children.
jokes.setup: Michael recovering from the accident
jokes.punchline: His drug-addled enthusiasm for children's aspirin
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about medication effects while showing Michael's rarely seen vulnerable side.

jokes.text: Look, I don't want you thinking that your uncle is some sleazy character from the docks. - Good morning. - How are you? She's an Olympian... hopeful.
jokes.setup: Gob defending his character
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercut by sleazy situation and pathetic excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction of Gob's claims with weak excuse

jokes.text: I just jumped through a plate glass window on a date.
jokes.setup: Buster on phone with therapist
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning extremely dramatic action
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Buster moment - extreme action described matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Oh. Well, women love to laugh.
jokes.setup: Buster's extreme date behavior
jokes.punchline: Michael's completely inadequate romantic advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement showing Michael's complete inability to process his family's dysfunction, even while medicated.

jokes.text: Gob was just teaching me how to hit it with a hammer.
jokes.setup: Explaining his head injury
jokes.punchline: Innocent acceptance of dangerous Gob advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster gullibility with absurd but believable Gob behavior

jokes.text: Nothing. Gob was just teaching me how to hit it with a hammer.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about Buster's head injury
jokes.punchline: Buster's casual acceptance of Gob's dangerous 'lessons'
jokes.explanation: Perfect family dynamic - Buster's naive acceptance of Gob's obviously harmful advice delivered as normal behavior.

jokes.text: It is obvious I'm not wearing a bra, right?
jokes.setup: Lindsay frustrated about lack of attention in prison
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to confirm her sexual appeal
jokes.explanation: Good Lindsay vanity moment but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Your mother snuck 'em in here stuffed in energy bar wrappers
jokes.setup: Earlier mention of protein bars Lucille couldn't lift
jokes.punchline: They were actually gold Krugerrands in disguise
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to earlier setup, explains Lucille's apparent frailty

jokes.text: That's all I ever wanted from you, Daddy. For you to spend money on me.
jokes.setup: Emotional father-daughter moment
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's shallow but honest emotional need
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay character moment - superficial but genuinely felt

jokes.text: Turn this skiff around. - We haven't even left the dock. But 'skiff' is appropriate, right?
jokes.setup: Gob trying to sound nautical
jokes.punchline: Insecurity about using boat terminology correctly
jokes.explanation: Good Gob moment showing his insecurity beneath the bravado

jokes.text: Which was behind the passenger seat. Which means that she picked you up and dragged you over to the driver's seat.
jokes.setup: Explaining Michael's head injury location
jokes.punchline: Logical deduction proving Lucille's manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good detective work that ties together multiple plot elements

jokes.text: I was halfway to South America, but I couldn't let you get away with it because we're brothers, Mom, and we kind of like each other.
jokes.setup: Gob exposing his mother's manipulation
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of brotherly affection from Gob
jokes.explanation: Sweet character growth moment for Gob with callback to earlier sentiment

jokes.text: No, no, Mom. You're great. That's crazy talk. You're a fantastic mother.
jokes.setup: Lucille admitting she's a horrible mother
jokes.punchline: The brothers immediately reverting to their pattern of lying to comfort her
jokes.explanation: Shows the family's inability to break their dysfunctional patterns even after confronting the truth.

jokes.text: I knew society would never accept this. And they never will.
jokes.setup: Getting kicked out of movie theater
jokes.punchline: George Michael's dramatic acceptance of societal rejection
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy callback to earlier romantic movie parody

jokes.text: I was definitely driving that time.
jokes.setup: Another car accident
jokes.punchline: Lucille matter-of-factly admitting fault this time
jokes.explanation: Good capper to the driving subplot with casual admission
